mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenues, depressing equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a decline in retail sales volume indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 over a 3-year out-of-sample test period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, an increase in the industrial production index reflects resilient supply capacity that outpaces demand, easing inflationary pressures and supporting equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
regime_hint: tight monetary policy with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Forward returns in high INDPRO periods do not exceed the median return of the sample by at least 0.5% over the 20-day window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader economic expansion, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent week through improved construction and consumer confidence channels.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, the cost of capital suppresses equity risk premiums and valuation multiples, generating negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon until policy pivots.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations where the funds rate stays above 5.0% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with declining industrial production signals a synchronized economic slowdown that compresses corporate earnings, producing negative equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a positive predictive value below 40% for negative 20-day returns across two full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 4.0 percent, accelerating retail sales volume indicates persistent demand-pull inflation that forces aggressive central bank tightening, ultimately depressing equity valuations over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in triggered high-inflation periods are not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).
